anchor.prefix: little sparks are the ATP streaming out:
anchor.quote: drag to rotate · scroll to zoom An interactive mitochondrion. The coral folds are the cristae — the pleated inner membrane where the electron transport chain charges ATP. (Renders offline; pauses when off-screen.)
anchor.suffix: How the charging actually works — the da

comments.body: Can you double check If this animation looks correct? If not make it bit more detailed.
comments.reply: Checked and rebuilt the 3D mitochondrion for accuracy and detail: the cristae are now proper lamellar inner-membrane shelves stacked across the matrix (was sparse floating rings), each ATP synthase is a full F1F0 lollipop (F0 stalk + F1 head poking into the matrix), and the matrix now holds mtDNA nucleoids and ribosomes so it reads as a living compartment. Verified it renders and orbits offline.
